sourceMovie.title: Selena Gomez: My Mind & Me
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Selena Gomez: My Mind & Me works as a documentary movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The documentary and music blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Last Waltz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Selena Gomez: My Mind & Me clicked for you, The Last Waltz (1978) is a strong next stop: both trade in documentary and music, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Selena Gomez: My Mind & Me?
faq.answer: Try The Last Waltz, The Greatest Night in Pop, Supersonic—each shares DNA with Selena Gomez: My Mind & Me while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Selena Gomez: My Mind & Me?
faq.answer: Selena Gomez: My Mind & Me (2022) is best described as Documentary, Music. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Selena Gomez: My Mind & Me worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this documentary lane.
